metadata.title: Biscuit Is Gone!
metadata.one_liner: The classroom hamster has escaped, and someone let him out on purpose!

narrative.premise: Biscuit the hamster has disappeared from his cage in Room 4! The latch was found open, so someone must have let him out. Can you talk to the suspects and search the school to find out who opened Biscuit's cage?
starting_knowledge: Biscuit the hamster is missing from his cage in the classroom.
The cage latch was found open - it did not break by itself.
Biscuit went missing during the lunch break.

locations.name: Classroom
locations.description: Your bright classroom with colourful posters on the walls. Biscuit's cage sits on a shelf near the window. The little door is wide open!
clues: A small hamster food pellet is on the floor beside the cage.
The cage latch is open. It has a tricky sideways mechanism that most people don't know about.

locations.name: Hallway
locations.description: A long corridor with rows of colourful coat pegs. It smells like school dinners.
clues: Miss Lee is standing here with most of the class. She says everyone was in the hallway after lunch except for a few children who had permission to be elsewhere.

locations.name: Garden
locations.description: A small school garden with raised flower beds. The soil is soft and dark.
clues: Tiny little paw prints in the muddy soil near the sunflower bed!
Biscuit himself is sniffing around happily near the sunflowers.

locations.name: Art Room
locations.description: A messy, wonderful room full of paint pots and coloured paper. There is wet paint drying on several boards.
clues: Lucas's large painting is propped up and still visibly wet. He has clearly been here for a while.

characters.appearance: A girl with straight dark hair and bright blue eyes. There is a small hamster food pellet stuck to the pocket of her green cardigan.
characters.background: Ruby is the class pet helper this week. She knows exactly how the cage latch works and adores Biscuit more than any other student.
characters.personality: Kind and animal-loving, but gets very defensive when she thinks she is in trouble.
characters.initial_attitude_towards_investigator: Overly friendly and very keen to talk about Oscar instead.
characters.mystery_action_real: She opened the cage during lunch break to play with Biscuit. He wriggled free and ran into the garden before she could stop him.
characters.stated_alibi: I was in the Art Room painting for the whole of lunch break!
characters.motive: She loves Biscuit very much and just wanted to cuddle him outside of official cage time.
knowledge: I know how to open the cage latch - you push it sideways first.
Biscuit loves running around outside his cage.

characters.appearance: A stocky boy with freckles and messy brown hair. He is always fidgeting with something.
characters.background: Oscar complained last week that Biscuit was too loud and kept him awake on the class sleepover.
characters.personality: Grumpy on the outside but fair on the inside. Gets cross when people blame him for things he didn't do.
characters.initial_attitude_towards_investigator: A little bit annoyed, but willing to talk.
characters.mystery_action_real: He was in the hallway the whole lunch break with the rest of the class and Miss Lee.
characters.stated_alibi: I was in the hallway with everyone else the whole time. Ask Miss Lee!
characters.motive: He complained that Biscuit was annoying. Some children think he might have let the hamster out on purpose.
knowledge: I was in the hallway with Miss Lee and the others the whole lunch break.
I didn't touch that cage. I don't even like hamsters.

characters.appearance: A tall, thin boy with paint-stained hands and a cheerful smile. He always has a paintbrush tucked behind one ear.
characters.background: Lucas loves art more than anything and spends every lunch break in the Art Room with permission from the teacher.
characters.personality: Easy-going and honest. Very proud of his paintings.
characters.initial_attitude_towards_investigator: Happy to chat and very helpful.
characters.mystery_action_real: He was in the Art Room painting for the whole of lunch break.
characters.stated_alibi: I was in the Art Room the whole time. My painting proves it - it's still wet!
knowledge: I was in the Art Room the whole lunch break. Ruby never came in.
My painting was still drying so I could not leave anyway.

ground_truth.what_happened: Ruby, who was the class pet helper, opened Biscuit's cage during lunch break to play with him. Biscuit escaped into the school garden before she could catch him.
ground_truth.why_it_happened: Ruby adored Biscuit and could not resist opening the cage for a cuddle, even though it was not allowed.
timeline: 12:00 PM - Lunch break begins. Miss Lee takes the class to the hallway.
12:05 PM - Ruby slips back into the classroom while the others are in the hallway.
12:10 PM - Ruby opens Biscuit's cage. A food pellet falls from her pocket. Biscuit hops out and runs toward the garden.
12:15 PM - Lucas settles into the Art Room to paint. Ruby is not with him.
12:45 PM - Lunch break ends. The class returns and discovers Biscuit is gone.
